Prince George’s and Montgomery Counties","items":[{"name":"Prince George's County Permits and Inspections","total":"10,566","zoneMultiplier":"0.94","priceNotApplicable":false,"priceHidden":true,"permanentId":-1,"zoneAdjustedPrice":"$10,566","catelogId":"1-3-1","longDescription":"

DISCLOSURES:

  • Permits and inspections item does not include any hoa related tasks. Please refer to items 1-1-5. HOA help. Contractor will submit specs and all necessary forms to HOA and 1-1-4. HOA help. Contractor will submit specs to Homeowner only
  • In case permits office requests a plot plan—which occurs in about 5% of cases depending on property details and scope items such as egress windows and walk-outs—the client is responsible for providing a plot plan stamped by a licensed surveyor. If the client does not already have a stamped plot plan in the surveyor package received when the house was purchased, the client may either hire a licensed surveyor directly or have BasementRemodeling.com obtain the plot plan at an average cost of $400–$800.

Homes built in the U.S. up until the early 1970s commonly used aluminum wiring and other outdated electrical systems. In previously finished basements within homes from this era, the presence of such wiring always require extensive electrical cleanup and rewiring to meet modern safety standards and codes.

Fixture is not included. - $230 - Dedicated line for fridge/freezer/sump pump in the basement. $350 - Paint existing exterior doors. 2x for double $150","trades":[],"hasCustomPricing":false,"omitFromPDF":false,"total":"47,990","price":"N/A","internalNotes":null,"zoneAdjustedPrice":"$42,904","name":"Basement Base Price","quantity":"783"},{"zoneAdjustedPrice":"N/A","longDescription":"

All basement walls and bulkheads per Owner's request and provided sketches. (2x4 lumber studs with treated sill plates, 16 inches on center). Ceiling bulk heads framed per existing site conditions (framing around all existing plumbing, mechanical and electrical utilities ran below floor joists). Base price may include one space (room) and one finished closet space per 500 sq. ft. of finished area.

SHELVING IS NOT INCLUDED WITH THE CLOSETS, SINCE EACH OF OUR CLIENTS HAS DIFFERENT PREFERENCES ON THE SHELVING. REFER TO ITEMS 3-13-1. Add closet single wire shelf (up to 6 ft.) THROUGH 3-13-5. Add wire shelving ClosetMaid system into the closet: 7-ft. – 10-ft.

OWNER INITIALS: ____________

Fixture is not included. Dedicated line for fridge/freezer/sump pump in the basement.","additionalInfo":null,"name":"Electrical Rough-in and Finish. All electrical work to be performed in accordance with state and local codes.","longDescription":"
  • IC-Rated 6'' Recessed Lights or Flushmount LED lights for bulkheads/closets etc - location per Owner's request. QTY: EXP[ math.ceil(sqFt / 50) ]EXP
  • For additional light refer to Item #(additio)
  • Stairway flush mount light. Fixture is not included.
  • Dedicated line for fridge/freezer/sump pump in the basement.
  • LED light bulbs 2700k
  • Cable/low voltage - location per Owner's request. QTY: EXP[ 1 if sqFt < 1000 else math.ceil(sqFt / 1000) ]EXP
  • Toggle (not Decora) switches/outlets per code (3- and 4-way where required)
  • Basic plastic or steel toggle (not Decora) switch/outlet plates (white, almond or ivory). PAINTING THE PLATES NOT INCLUDED.
  • Basement smoke and Combo (smoke + CO) detectors will be installed per code.
  • Mechanical/unfinished area lighting is included:
    • Areas under 16 sq. ft. (4'x4') - pull light only (lamp holder with pull) (add switch 20$)
    • Areas over 16 sq. ft. - Switch and one utility light (lamp holder) per 100 sq. ft. (Additional. Light - 20$)

After Project completion Owner will need to contact Owner's cable/internet service provider to connect newly installed low voltage lines to Owner's provider's equipment/lines. Due to some implications that may be caused by interfering with Owner's provider's equipment, Contractor is unable to perform this connection.

Level 4 is the Used by every non-custom home builder in the country. Used by 90% of custom home builders in the country. Joints are to follow a three-coat system, consisting of; (1) a tape coat plus (2) two subsequent coats applied over the tape coat and fastener heads. All joint compounds should be sanded to a smooth finish free of tool marks and ridges. Drywall surface itself is not skim-coated.

Level 4 with Satin Paint: You may see joint lines, uneven textures, or minor flaws in lighting-sensitive areas.

OWNER INITIALS: ____________

  • ANY DOORS UNDER 80'' WILL BE BILLED EXTRA. REFER TO ITEM 1-9-19. Custom height shorter door (less than 80”) upcharge. OWNER INITIALS: ______________

  • STANDARD Doors (See doors page below for included styles), UNLESS STATED OTHERWISE. QTY: EXP[ 4 if sqFt < 1000 else math.floor( (sqFt + 125) / 250) ]EXP

  • STANDARD 2 ¼ Door/Window trim - WM-327, WM-472 and WM-371 PROFILE ONLY, UNLESS STATED OTHERWISE. Does not include rosettes (additonal corner piece) or plinth block (additonal piece at the base of the door casing).

  • STANDARD 3 ¼ Baseboard - WM-623, WM-432 or WM-663 PROFILES ONLY, UNLESS STATED OTHERWISE.

  • Basic pine stair rail (banister) painted white semi-gloss WM-240 PROFILE ONLY, UNLESS STATED OTHERWISE

  • Wrap all basement columns with one-piece chair rail WM-300 PROFILE ONLY and one-piece crown molding WM-49 PROFILE ONLY, UNLESS STATED OTHERWISE

  • $30 door lock allowance per each door included

PLEASE NOTE, IT IS OWNER'S RESPONSIBILITY TO UNDERSTAND WHAT DOOR AND TRIM PROFILES ARE INCLUDED. PLEASE “GOOGLE” PROFILE NAMES FOR MORE INFORMATION.

Can be exchanged for Painted floor or steps.","price":"N/A"},{"total":"N/A","priceHidden":true,"additionalInfo":null,"excludeFromCustomPricingZone":false,"name":"Painting details - General Specs","internalNotes":null,"catelogId":"1-9-24","permanentId":8,"longDescription":"

GENERAL SPECS:

  • Filling and caulking all nail-holes and gaps.

  • One coat of Contractor's grade primer on all walls and ceiling

  • Sherwin Williams 200. WHITE ceiling paint and FLAT COLORED paint on the walls

  • Paint existing exterior door(s)

    • PAINT FINISH UPGRADE TO SATIN/EGGSHELL WILL RESUL IN EXTRA COST OF $4/SQ. FT. due to the following reasons: 1. Level 5 drywall finishing required; 2. High sheen finishes cost more per gallon; 3. Touching up high sheen finishes will require painting the entire wall; 4. Flat paint evens out imperfections that might show with high sheen finishes, particularly the drywall joints. This results a lot in additional work; 5. Working with high sheen finishes requires more expertise and experience overall.

  • White Sherwin Williams 200 SEMI-GLOSS paint for doors and trims

  • CONTINGENCIES:

    • All ceilings to be white (colored ceiling - extra cost)
    • Deep dark colors (more than 2 coats of paint are necessary) will result in extra cost
    • Accent wall - extra cost
    • Custom painting will result in extra cost
    • 1 COLOR PER ROOM. MORE THAN ONE COLOR PER ROOM WILL RESULT IN EXTRA COST - PAINTING OF EXISTING WOOD WINDOWS IS NOT INCLUDED. PLEASE REFER TO ITEM 1-9-27. Paint existing full size wooden windows

OWNER INITIALS: ______________

Under certain circumstances, the contractor may agree to credit the “floor leveling” item and not perform any subfloor leveling prior to installation. In such cases, the customer acknowledges and agrees to the following:

  • Warranty Impact: Skipping floor leveling will likely void the flooring manufacturer’s warranty.
  • Structural Concerns: The floor may feel soft, hollow, or uneven underfoot due to the underlying concrete slab irregularities.
  • Separation & Gaps: The flooring may begin to separate at the seams or develop visible gaps over time.
  • Movement & Shifting: Uneven subfloors can cause the floating floor to shift, creak, or move under pressure.
  • Excessive Wear: Stress points caused by uneven support may cause damage to the locking mechanisms between planks.
  • Moisture Trapping: Low spots in the slab or subfloor may collect moisture, leading to swelling, warping, or mold/mildew issues beneath the flooring.
  • esthetic Defects: Visible height differences, lippage, or “bounce” may occur, negatively affecting the look and feel of the finished floor.

By accepting a credit in place of floor leveling, the customer understands these risks and releases the contractor from responsibility for any problems that arise from the installation of flooring over an unlevel surface.
